Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
Robert D. winston
city Attorney
City of Mt. Shasta
PoO. BOX 263
Mt. Shasta′  CA

Re:  Your Request for lnformal Assistance
Our Fi■e No. I-90-230
Dear Mr. Winston:
This is in response to your letter requesting assistance on
behalf of Mt. Shasta Planning commissioner John Guill concerning
his respons ibi l ities under the conflict-of- interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the rraglrr1.1/ since your iequest does
not concern a specific decision, but is instead a request for
generaL gu.idance concerni-ng the plannj'ng commissioner's
iespons iri I ities, 
we are treating your request as one for informal
.=sistance . 2 /
OUESTION
May Planning Commissioner Guill participate in planning com-
mission decisions concerning clients represented by the
commissioner's employer, Stevens and Associates?
CONCLUSION
Planning Commissioner Guill may participate in decisions
concerning alplicants represented by the planning commissioner's
1-/ Government Code Sections 81Ooo-91015. AIl statutory refer-
ences are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
com-

Section 18600, et seq.- A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division o oi the California code of Regulations'
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor wi'th the
i. ""ii, 
lrovj.ded by an opinion oi formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3) ')
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 . sacramento cA 958O4.O8O7 . (916) 322'5660


File No. T-90-230
Page 2
empLoyer, stevens and Associates
have a material financial effect
Stevens personallY.
provided the decisions wiLl not
on Stevens and Associates or Mr.
FACTS
planning Coromissioner GUiII is an employee of stevens and
Associates, an architectural firm that does business in the city
of Mt. Shasta. once the firrn has comPleted architectural pJ,ans
for their clients, many of the clients appear before the planning
commission seeking approval of building plans. Neither Stevens
and Associates, nor the planning commissioner's compensation is
contingent on the apProval of the plan by the planning conmission.
Howevei, frequently, the owner of stevens and Associates
represents the firm's clients before the planningT commission and
Planning Commissioner Gui11.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 Prohibits any public official, 
whether ap-
pointed or elected, fron rnaking, participating in making, or
btherwise using his official 
position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official 
has a financial interest- 
A
ttpublic officialrt is defined in Section 82048 to include every
rnEmber, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
goverrunent agency, including members of planning commissions-
Section 87103 specifies that an officiat 
has a financial
interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material f j.nancial effect, distinguishable
from the effect on the public Aenerally, on the official 
or a
rnember of his or her imrnediate fanily or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institu-
tion in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to off icj-a1
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
(9250) or nore in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the tine e/hen the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
section 87Lo3 (c) and (d) .
consequently, stevens and Associates is an econonic interest
because the firn ernploys Comrnissioner Guil1 and because Stevens
and Associates has presumabJ.y been a source of income to the plan-
ning connissioner of over $250 in the last 12 nonths. Thus, the
planning conmissioner is required to disqualify hinself fron plan-
-ning coromission decisions where it is reasonably foreseeable that


File No. l-9o-23o
Page 3
the decision !/i11 have a material financial effect on Stevens and
Associates, distinguishable from the effect on the public gener-
a1ly.
whether the financial consequences of a decision are reason-
ably foreseeable at the tine a glovernmental decision is rnade
depends on the facts of each particular case. An effect is
coirsidered reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial
likelihood that it will occur. certainty is not required.
Horrrever, if an effect is only a nere possibility, 
it is not
reasonably foreseeabl,e. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198,
copy enclosed. )
The commission has adopted differing guidelines to deterrnine
whether an effect is nateriat, depending on the specific
circumstances of each decision. For example, where a source of
income is directly before the planning cornmission as an applicant
or the subject of the decision, Regutation 1a7o2.1(a) (1) (copy
enclosed) provj.des that the effect of the decision on a source of
incone is deened material and disqua l ification is required-
(conbs Advice Letter, No. A-89-L77, copy enclosed.)
A source of incorne is directly before the planning cornmission
lrhen the source initiates the proceedinq by filing an application,
cIaim, appeal, or similar request, or is a named party in, or the
subject oi, tne proceeding. A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance,
ren6wal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or
other entitlenent to, or contract with, the subject person or
business entity. (Regulation La102.1(b).)
According to your facts the owner of Stevens and Associates
will come before the ptanning conmission in his representative
capacity in conjunction with the projects of the firm's clients.
In analyzing a sinilar situation we concluded that there a source
of incoire appears before a planning commission in a representative
capacity, as an agent of an unrelated business entity, and the
property subject to the decision j.s the property of _the unrelated
Lusiness entity, the source of income is not appearing directly
before the planning cornmission. (Gi11 Advice Letter, No. A-85-
252, copy enclosed.) Thus, so long as the owner of stevens and
associatEs did not initiate the proceeding by filing 
an applica-
tion, c1ain, appeal, or similar request, or is a named party in,
or the subject of, the proceeding we conclude that stevens and
Associates is not directly involved and consequently' autonatic
disqual ification would not be required.
Ho$rever, even where a source of income is not directly before
the planning cornmission, the comnissioner nust stiIl 
disqualify
nirnsetf where Stevens and Associates or it's 
ov/ner erilI be


File No. I-9 0-2 3 o
Page 4
indirectLy naterially affected.3/ whether an effect on a business
entity is material is deterroj.ned by exanining the magnitude of the
foreseeabLe financial effect on the source of incorne pursuant to
Regulation L8702.2 (copy enclosed). Regulation La702.2 provides
that whether the indirect effect on a business entity is naterial
depends on the financial size of the busj.ness entity. 
For a
relatively snatl business entity, the indirect effect of a deci-
sion is material where:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $10,000 or nore; or
(2) The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or elininating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of $2,500 or morei or
(3) The decision wi.1l result in the increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of 910,000 or more.
where a source of income is a person and not directly before
the city council, as in the case of Mr. stevens, Regulation
La7o2.6 provides:
The effect of a decision is materia] as to an
individual who is a source of income or gifts to an
official 
if any of the following applies:
(a) The decision will affect the
individual's income, investments, or other
tangible or intangible assets or liabilities
(other than real property) bY $1,000 or morei
or
indiVl:とalTtter::Ii:l::eytllil[[[:[t li a man_
ner that is considered material under Section
■8702.3 or Section 
■8702.4.
3/  。n May .。′
and Assoc■ateS
and AssoC■ateS
income to the
No. A-80-069′
1990 you explained that Mr. Stevens owns stevens
as a sole proprietorship. 
As sole owner of Stevens
Mr. stevens would also be treated as a source of
planning commissioner. (Hentschke Advice Letter,
copy enclosed. )


File No. r-9 o-2 3 o
Page 5
Thus, absent facts indicating a foreseeable financial impact
on stevens and Associates or ttr. stevens personall,y as a result of
the decision, we would conclude that Planning Com:nissioner Guill
uray participate in the decisions concerning applicants represented
by t{r . stevens .
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
Sincerely,
scott HaIlabrin
General counsel
I, Legal Division
